Title: Manna From Heaven
A Bold Opportunity
Ashes and Opportunities
A Financial Windfall
Crash and Earn
Description:
A tremendous opportunity seems to have landed... well crashed in our favor. A Hurston satellite has quite ungracefully plummeted to the surface. Thankfully, it appears that a Prototype TDC-XX Blade has miraculously survived the incident.

Now, I know that there are plenty of Hurston's rivals who would be... eager... to get an advance look at this tech. I'm sure I could convince them to pay handsomely for an opportunity like this, but I'll need you to retrieve it. You can start your search at the Crash Site and work out from there. 

Once recovered, you will only have a short time to upload the information from the Prototype TDC-XX Blade using the decryption software on a designated Hurston server before its security protocols cause it to self-destruct. Your best bet will be to access a data center inside one of the storage facilities that dot the surface of the planet. They typically have minimal security so I don't foresee you running into any issues. 

Needless to say, I will pay you handsomely upon completion and we can both bask in knowledge that Hurston has been hit where it hurts.

-cd

[Update]

I managed to intercept an interesting bit of news coming over the comms. It seems Hurston's fortunes are falling quite literally; one of their satellites has crashed to the surface. Not all is lost however, for the valuable Prototype TDC-XX Blade onboard appears to have survived the impact.  

Now, this is one of those moments where enterprising individuals face a choice. Either do nothing, let Hurston reclaim their property and let this wretched world keep turning or seize the opportunity to capitalize. Personally, I'm leaning towards the latter and I'm hoping you feel the same. 

If you were to obtain the part from the [location] before Hurston does, I'm sure I could find some parties interested in paying handsomely for a sneak peek at Hurston's hard work.

One catch (as always), is that in order to safely decrypt and extract the data, you must connect it to an official Hurston server before its security protocols cause it to self-destruct. I recommend using a storage facilities data terminal. They tend to be easier to access than anything that can be found in Lorville proper. Once the transfer is complete, I will issue your just rewards.

Best of luck,

-cd

[Update]

My Dear Compatriot,

Wonderful tidings. It seems that we have received an unexpected but most welcomed guest - a Hurston satellite has been ejected from orbit and crashed practically straight into our laps. Most importantly, the Prototype TDC-XX Blade onboard has survived intact.  

Now, it just so happens that this cutting-edge Prototype has been long desired by some deep-pocketed associates of mine. If you were able to acquire this wayward Blade from the Crash Site before Hurston manages to do so, I could ensure significant restitution for your time and troubles.

The only real wrinkle is that the information on the Prototype is inaccessible (and will self-destruct) unless connected to an official Hurston server. Fortunately, there is access to the said server dotted around the planet inside Hurston Storage Facilities. It should be short work for you to get the Blade and then take it to the server for decryption and extraction. Once the data transfer is finished, I will happily transfer the well-earned credits into your account. 

Safe travels,

-cd

[Update]
